title: Add CLI option for recursive constant function depth limit (CONST_FUNC_RECURSION_MAX)
body:
### Description

`CONST_FUNC_RECURSION_MAX` (V3Simulate.h:78) is hardcoded to 1000.
When a recursive constant function exceeds this depth, Verilator silently
gives up on constant folding with no way for the user to increase the limit.

There is already a `--module-recursion-depth` switch for recursive modules.
A similar `--func-recursion-depth` would let users with deep recursive
constant functions raise the limit without patching the source.

Note: `CALL_STACK_MAX = 100` on line 79 is only used for error message
truncation (not an execution limit), so it does not need a CLI option.

### Current behavior

```
static constexpr int CONST_FUNC_RECURSION_MAX = 1000;  // V3Simulate.h:78
```

When exceeded, `clearOptimizable()` is called and the function is not
constant-folded. Depending on context this may cause downstream errors
(e.g. localparam not being constant).

### Suggested improvement

Add a `--func-recursion-depth <N>` option (default 1000) that
replaces the hardcoded constant, similar to `--module-recursion-depth`.

### Version

Verilator 5.044 2026-01-01 rev v5.044-52-g7e7b6d7fb
